title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language magazine:

'Art and music in schools: necessary or a luxury? With schools under increasing pressure to focus on academic subjects, some argue that art and music should be compulsory, while others say they are less important. Write an article sharing your views.'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
The Art of Being Human: Why Creativity Must Stay in Schools

In the ongoing debate about what our schools should prioritise, the arts are consistently among the first subjects to face cuts. This is, I would argue, a profoundly shortsighted approach — one that misunderstands both the nature of creativity and its vital role in a well-rounded education.

Art and music develop skills that extend far beyond the studio or the concert hall. They cultivate patience, resilience, self-expression, and the ability to think laterally — qualities that are extraordinarily valuable in almost every professional field. Research consistently shows that students who participate in the arts perform better academically overall, not worse.

Furthermore, for many children, art and music represent the only area of school life in which they feel genuinely competent and engaged. Remove these subjects, and you risk disengaging a significant proportion of students entirely.

A narrow curriculum focused exclusively on maths and sciences may produce exam results, but it will not produce well-rounded, creative, or emotionally intelligent adults. Compulsory arts education is not a luxury — it is a necessity.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your English friend Chris is visiting your city and you want to recommend something cultural to do. Write an email to Chris recommending an art exhibition, concert, or theatre performance you have recently attended or heard about, explaining what it involves, why you think Chris would enjoy it, and providing any practical information they might need.

Write your email to Chris.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Chris,

I am so glad you are coming to visit — I have been thinking about what we should do together, and I have the perfect suggestion!

There is an incredible photography exhibition currently running at the City Arts Centre called 'Unseen Worlds', which features the work of twelve international photographers exploring life in remote communities. I went last week and was completely captivated — the images are stunning, emotionally powerful, and unlike anything I have seen locally before.

I genuinely think you would love it. You mentioned once that you are interested in travel and different cultures, and this exhibition really captures that spirit beautifully.

Practically speaking, it runs until the end of next month, so we have plenty of time. Entry costs around twelve pounds for adults, though there is a student discount if you have your university card. The gallery is open Tuesday to Sunday from ten in the morning until six in the evening.

Let me know which day suits you best and I will book tickets in advance to avoid queuing.

Can't wait to see you!

Lucia

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt:
Your school has asked you to write a report on the findings of a survey about students' participation in creative activities outside the classroom. Include information about which activities students currently take part in, what barriers prevent greater participation, and recommendations for how the school could better support creative engagement.

Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Student Participation in Creative Activities at Ferndale School

Introduction
This report is based on survey responses from 200 students and examines their engagement with creative activities such as music, visual art, drama, photography, and creative writing outside school hours.

Current Participation Levels
The survey found that 62% of students participate in at least one creative activity in their own time, with music and visual art being the most popular choices. However, participation rates drop significantly among students in examination years, suggesting that academic pressure discourages creative engagement at precisely the age when many students are developing their individual identities.

Barriers to Participation
The most commonly cited barriers were lack of time (58%), financial cost of materials or lessons (44%), and limited access to suitable spaces (29%). Several students expressed a desire to participate but felt there were insufficient affordable options available locally.

Recommendations
The school should establish a free after-school creative club with access to art materials, musical instruments, and digital equipment. Partnerships with local arts organisations could provide professional mentoring at minimal cost. Reducing creative homework during peak exam periods would also help alleviate pressure on students.
